assertions.title: Peer Policies card lists exactly the current group's configured peers

steps.action: Run assigned vitest command against peer policies suites
steps.observed: 3 test files ran and passed (19/19 tests).

steps.action: Evaluate src/screens/DashboardScreen/states/__tests__/PoliciesState.peerRowParity.test.tsx case: "Peer Policies card renders exactly one row per configured peer (VAL-POLICIES-001)"
steps.expected: Rendered .policies-peer-name row count equals runtimeStatus.peer_permission_states length
steps.observed: Assertion passed; row count matched configured peer permission states.

evidence.consoleErrors: N/A (vitest surface). One known pre-existing nested <button> warning was emitted to stderr during PeersPanel render.

assertions.title: Peer Policies row order tracks PeerRow indices

steps.action: Evaluate parity test case: "both surfaces surface identical allowed-verb state per peer/verb"
steps.expected: Peer Policies rows and PeerRow entries resolve against the same peer idx identity
steps.observed: Test iterated peers by idx, located matching "Peer #idx" and "#idx" rows, and verified parity for each peer/verb without mismatch.

steps.action: Evaluate Dashboard policies view smoke rows
steps.expected: Peer Policies list renders indexed peer labels for roster
steps.observed: DashboardPolicies test confirmed rows for Peer #0, #1, #2 in the rendered list.

assertions.title: Granted badge tones match Paper reference

steps.action: Inspect Peer Policies runtime rendering in PoliciesState
steps.observed: PoliciesState maps chips to tone="success" (SIGN), tone="info" (ECDH), tone="ping" (PING), tone="onboard" (ONBOARD).

steps.action: Inspect permission badge tone styles in src/styles/global.css
steps.expected: Tone classes resolve to green/cyan/purple/amber styled variants with dedicated border/background values
steps.observed: .permission-badge, .permission-badge.info, .permission-badge.ping, and .permission-badge.onboard define distinct tone color/border/background styles.

steps.action: Confirm associated peer policy rendering tests pass
steps.expected: No regression in badge render paths

assertions.title: Ungranted badges render as muted outline

steps.expected: PoliciesState chip has .muted when effective policy does not allow
steps.observed: Test explicitly asserts chipBadge.classList.contains("muted") === !allowed for each peer/method.
PeerRow tests for deny and ask/unset states passed, confirming ungranted representation behavior.

assertions.title: PoliciesState and PeerRow stay in sync via effective_policy

steps.action: Run cross-surface parity test for PoliciesState + PeersPanel
steps.expected: For every peer/method tuple, both surfaces agree on allowed/ungranted state from effective_policy.request
steps.observed: Parity test iterated all peers and methods, deriving allowed via resolveRequestPolicyAllows and confirming PoliciesState chip muted state matches PeerRow badge presence.

assertions.title: PeerRow badges derive from effective_policy, not heuristics

steps.action: Evaluate PeerRow effective policy tests with heuristic mismatch
steps.expected: When effective_policy.request.sign=deny and can_sign=true, SIGN badge remains ungranted
steps.observed: Test "omits SIGN badge when effective_policy.request.sign=deny even though peer.can_sign=true" passed.

steps.action: Evaluate runtime-mode parity with permissionState present
steps.expected: Badge set follows effective_policy grants even for offline peers in runtime mode
steps.observed: Offline runtime-mode test passed and shows all effective-policy-granted badges.

assertions.title: ONBOARD chip reflects onboarding grant, not hard-coded to peer #1

steps.action: Evaluate PeerRow/PoliciesState parity matrix with varied peer indices and onboarding grants
steps.expected: ONBOARD display follows effective_policy.request.onboard per peer, independent of idx
steps.observed: Parity test includes peers idx 0-3 with mixed onboard allow/deny and asserts badge presence equals resolveRequestPolicyAllows outcome for each peer.

steps.expected: ONBOARD appears only when effective policy grants it
steps.observed: PeerRow tests passed for both onboard-allow and onboard-deny/ungranted paths.

frictions.description: Targeted vitest run emits a React warning about nested <button> elements from PeersPanel while executing parity tests.
frictions.resolution: Treated as known pre-existing issue documented in mission AGENTS.md; continued assertion validation because tests still passed.

summary: Tested 7 assigned assertions for peer-policies view using the requested vitest suites; all 7 passed based on passing tests plus direct component/style observations, with one known pre-existing nested-button warning captured as non-blocking friction.
